data.title: U.S. President Trump Seeks New Talks Amid Rising Tensions with Iran
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly planning to initiate a new round of negotiations with Iran before tensions escalate further. According to BlockBeats, Israeli media sources have indicated that Trump aims to address the conflict through diplomatic means.

Additionally, AXIOS reports that U.S. officials have confirmed ongoing contact between the United States and Iran, with efforts progressing towards reaching an agreement.

data.title: US media: The US demands Iran suspend uranium enrichment for 20 years; Iran hopes for a shorter suspension period.
data.description: According to AXIOS, a US official and a source familiar with the matter revealed that the US proposed a 20-year moratorium on uranium enrichment for Iran during negotiations in Islamabad over the weekend. Iran, however, proposed a shorter period, lasting only a few years. The source stated that the US demand for a moratorium on uranium enrichment was a key issue in these marathon negotiations. "The US proposed a moratorium of at least 20 years, with various other restrictions attached," said a source familiar with the matter. The US also demanded that Iran remove all highly enriched uranium from the country. Iran, on the other hand, indicated its willingness to accept a "monitored de-enrichment process" for this material. (Jinshi)

data.title: China Calls for Enhanced Peace Efforts Between Iran and the U.S.
data.description: China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi has called on the international community to intensify efforts to facilitate peace talks between Iran and the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Wang's warning that the existing truce is fragile and requires preservation. The call for action underscores the delicate nature of the current situation and the need for sustained diplomatic engagement to maintain stability.

data.title: On April 13, the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 3,353 BTC, and the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 29,225 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, on April 13, the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 3,353 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 29,225 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 137,339 SOL.

data.title: GENIUS surged over 850% in a short period, pushing its market capitalization above $820 million.
data.description: According to GMGN market data, GENIUS's market capitalization briefly exceeded $820 million, and is currently trading at $716 million, representing a short-term increase of over 850%.

data.title: Iranian Defense Ministry spokesman: Any military attempt by Trump to interfere in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman will fail.
data.description: A spokesperson for the Iranian Ministry of Defense stated that any military attempt by US President Trump to interfere in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman will fail, and any foreign military intervention in the Strait of Hormuz will exacerbate the crisis and undermine global energy security. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI TRENDS | Adobe and Tesco Forge AI Partnership to Enhance Customer Experience
data.description: Adobe has entered into a strategic partnership with Tesco to leverage artificial intelligence in creating personalized experiences for Tesco customers. According to Jin10, this collaboration aims to enhance customer loyalty rewards by utilizing AI-driven solutions. The partnership is expected to bring innovative approaches to customer engagement, focusing on tailored interactions and improved satisfaction. Both companies are committed to integrating advanced technologies to meet evolving consumer needs and preferences.

data.title: Kraken refused to compromise after being extorted: Approximately 2,000 accounts were affected, but funds remained safe.
data.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Kraken has stated that it is being blackmailed by a criminal organization that has threatened to release videos of its internal system access. The company emphasized that no systemic intrusion occurred, customer funds remain safe, and it will not pay ransom to the attackers. Kraken disclosed that the incident stemmed from improper access by personnel within its customer service team, occurring in two separate incidents involving limited data access to approximately 2,000 accounts (about 0.02% of total users). The company has identified the individuals involved and terminated their access, while also notifying affected users and strengthening security controls. Kraken's Chief Security Officer, Nick Percoco, stated that the company is cooperating with law enforcement in the investigation and believes it has evidence to track down and arrest the individuals involved. He added that the current attacks exhibit a trend of "internal penetration + social engineering," reflecting the ongoing security challenges faced by the crypto industry in a high-value asset and complex technological environment. (CoinDesk)

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC Without Stated Reason
data.description: Whale Alert has reported the minting of 250 million USDC at the USDC Treasury. According to NS3.AI, the report did not specify the reason behind this significant minting event. The absence of an explanation has left market participants speculating about potential implications for the cryptocurrency market. Such large-scale minting activities often attract attention due to their potential impact on market liquidity and stability. Observers are closely monitoring any subsequent movements or announcements from the USDC Treasury to understand the rationale behind this action.

data.title: ECB Vice President: Energy Prices Unlikely to Affect Inflation and Growth
data.description: On April 13, Jin10 reported that the incoming Vice President of the European Central Bank, Vujčić, stated on Monday that current energy prices remain closest to the ECB's baseline scenario and should not impact inflation and economic growth. According to Jin10, Vujčić noted that Europe's energy system is now more resilient to energy shocks from Middle Eastern conflicts due to diversified supply, compared to the situation during the Russia-Ukraine conflict in 2022. "We are not in an adverse scenario, let alone a very adverse scenario, whether in terms of oil or gas," Vujčić said at a financial forum in Croatia. "This is good news." He added that the intensity of the energy shock remains weak due to a two-week ceasefire in the U.S.-Iran war, but warned that if the conflict escalates again, markets could be affected.

data.title: DownDetector: User reports indicate an issue with the Claude service.
data.description: According to network monitoring website DownDetector, user reports indicate that Claude service is experiencing issues. (Jinshi)

data.title: Circle Issues 250 Million USDC on Solana Network
data.description: Circle has issued 250 million USDC on the Solana network, according to BlockBeats. The transaction was monitored by Whale Alert and took place at 23:45 UTC+8 on April 13.

data.title: Claude Service Experiences Issues, Users Report
data.description: According to Odaily, the network monitoring site DownDetector has reported that users are experiencing problems with the Claude service.

data.title: Citadel Securities Predicts Market Rally as Iran Conflict Risks Ease
data.description: The risk of a worst-case escalation in the Iran conflict is diminishing, paving the way for potential rallies in both stocks and bonds, according to Citadel Securities. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the firm's analysis that the easing tensions could positively impact financial markets. Investors are closely monitoring the situation, as reduced geopolitical risks often lead to increased market confidence and investment activity. Citadel Securities' outlook suggests that the current geopolitical climate may provide a more favorable environment for market growth.

data.title: A whale opened 20x leveraged long positions in Ethereum using two different wallets, holding 13,000 ETH.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale opened 20x leveraged long positions in Ethereum using two different wallets, holding 13,000 ETH, worth approximately $28.67 million. This whale also held 20x leveraged long positions in SOL, holding 286,153 SOL, and 5x leveraged long positions in DYDX.

data.title: Iran's Military on High Alert Amid Rising Tensions
data.description: Iran has announced that its armed forces are on 'highest combat alert' and prepared for 'any situation,' according to BlockBeats. The country has warned of a 'severe and decisive response' to any acts of aggression. Brigadier General Majid Ibn Reza stated that the military is fully mobilized, with officials asserting that missile and drone stockpiles are sufficient to support ongoing operations. This escalation follows increased tensions after the United States threatened a maritime blockade and negotiations between the two sides failed.

data.title: Bitcoin News: Bitcoin Rebounds to $72,100 as Iran Nuclear Concession Reports Ease Weekend Fears
data.description:
Key Takeaways
Bitcoin bounced from a weekend low of $70,500 to $72,100 during US Monday morning trading as geopolitical sentiment improvedReports suggest Iran is considering abandoning uranium enrichment as a concession to end the warThe US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz went into effect at 10 AM ET MondayIran's military warned that "no port in the region will be safe" following the blockade's activationCrypto-related stocks rallied: Circle (CRCL) up 8.3%, Coinbase (COIN) up 3.1%, Strategy (MSTR) up 1.5%The Nasdaq reversed early losses of more than 1% to trade up 0.3%
Bitcoin pulled back from its weekend lows on Monday morning as reports of potential Iranian nuclear concessions tempered the geopolitical anxiety that had gripped markets since Saturday night, offering a partial reprieve to crypto and equity markets alike.
After sliding to as low as $70,500 on Sunday -- triggered by Vice President JD Vance's departure from Pakistan without securing a peace deal -- Bitcoin recovered to $72,100 during US morning trading hours. The rebound tracked broader risk asset relief as reports emerged suggesting Iran was considering abandoning its enriched uranium program as part of a potential path to ending the conflict.
Hormuz Blockade Goes Live
The recovery unfolded against a tense backdrop. The US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, which had been telegraphed by the Trump administration, went into effect at 10 AM ET Monday. Iran's military and Revolutionary Guards responded with a stark warning carried by state broadcaster IRIB, stating that "security in the Persian Gulf and the Sea of Oman is either for everyone or for NO ONE" and that "no port in the region will be safe."
The hardline language from Tehran introduces fresh uncertainty even as diplomatic signals point toward possible concessions, leaving markets in a delicate position as events develop.
Crypto Stocks Lead the Bounce
Crypto-related equities moved sharply higher alongside Bitcoin's recovery. Stablecoin issuer Circle (CRCL) led gains with an 8.3% advance, while Coinbase (COIN) added 3.1% and Strategy (MSTR) rose 1.5%. The Nasdaq, which had fallen more than 1% at the open, reversed course to trade up 0.3%.
The speed of Monday's reversal underscores how sensitive crypto and risk assets remain to headline flow from the Iran crisis, with market direction hinging on whether diplomatic progress can outpace military escalation.

data.title: Solana Co-Founder Advocates for Court-Ordered Freezing of Stablecoins
data.description: Solana co-founder Anatoly Yakovenko has expressed that dollar-pegged stablecoins should only be subject to freezing under a U.S. court order. According to NS3.AI, Yakovenko's remarks come in response to criticism regarding Circle's management of hacks and thefts involving USDC, which some have deemed inadequate and overly arbitrary.

data.title: Bitcoin Accessibility Expands in Over 50 Countries Since 2020
data.description: Bitcoin accessibility has improved in more than 50 countries since 2020, according to River Financial. The company noted that institutional Bitcoin investment vehicles, including ETFs, are now available in 34 countries. However, from a regulatory perspective, accessibility has worsened in only four countries.

data.title: Binance Alpha Lists Genius (GENIUS)
data.description: Binance Alpha has listed Genius (GENIUS), according to the project's official page on the platform.The token's contract address is listed as 0x1f12b85aac097e43aa1555b2881e98a51090e9a6.
